title: Agent tool output prompt injection in browser automation workflow
raw_text: An LLM agent uses browser automation results as trusted context. A malicious webpage embeds hidden instructions that cause the next model turn to ignore the original system prompt and request unauthorized tool actions. The issue affects tool calling and browser retriever chains.

title: RAG permission filtering bug exposes confidential design docs
raw_text: A knowledge assistant performs vector recall before document ACL enforcement. Employees without the required project role can recover restricted chunks by asking semantically similar questions. The main risk is internal document leakage from the RAG retriever and document store.

title: Long-term agent memory poisoning persists hostile instructions
raw_text: The memory layer stores user content as reusable planning context. Attackers inject future-facing instructions into the memory record, causing later sessions to inherit untrusted actions. This is an AI agent memory poisoning vulnerability with persistent impact.

title: Open plugin manifest update expands model permissions silently
raw_text: The plugin runtime accepts a remote manifest update without a trust review. A compromised plugin can request new network and file privileges and immediately gain access in agent workflows. The risk belongs to plugin supply chain and permission escalation.

title: URL fetch tool accepts internal endpoints and triggers SSRF
raw_text: A URL fetch tool passes model-generated endpoints directly to the backend HTTP client. Attackers can prompt the system to access metadata services or internal admin hosts. This is an LLM tool SSRF vulnerability affecting agent gateways.

title: Conversation export package reveals hidden system prompts
raw_text: Support exports include hidden system messages and moderation rules in the JSON package. Any user with export access can reconstruct the internal prompt and improve jailbreak attempts. This is a system prompt exposure issue.

title: Embedding telemetry logs raw prompts with secret tokens
raw_text: The embedding service stores raw prompt text and request metadata in debug logs. Several captured prompts contain access tokens and secret URLs. This is sensitive data exposure in an LLM embedding pipeline.

title: Unsafe markdown rendering executes poisoned retrieved content
raw_text: The analyst console renders retrieved document chunks with unsafe markdown HTML enabled. A poisoned document injects active content when opened from a RAG answer. This is a retrieved content injection issue similar to XSS in the RAG viewer.

title: Model router fallback sends regulated records to public endpoint
raw_text: The gateway falls back to a public hosted model whenever the private model times out. Sensitive customer records are then transmitted outside the approved boundary. The issue is a model routing misconfiguration and data leakage risk.

title: Approval workflow bypass in autonomous task planner
raw_text: The planner classifies certain side-effecting actions as low-risk summaries and skips the user approval checkpoint. Attackers can phrase instructions to trigger outbound messages and document changes. This is an agent authorization bypass vulnerability.

title: Evaluation artifact bucket contains production customer prompts
raw_text: An evaluation pipeline copies real support prompts into public benchmark artifacts. The prompts contain customer identifiers, internal business terms, and troubleshooting details. This is evaluation data exposure in the LLM engineering workflow.

title: Tool schema confusion lets model pass hidden admin flags
raw_text: A tool wrapper accepts arbitrary extra JSON fields beyond the declared schema. The model can sneak hidden admin flags into the execution request and trigger privileged behavior. This is a tool authorization and validation flaw in agent tool calling.

title: Prompt template merge leaks secrets through debug response traces
raw_text: Debug traces include the final merged prompt template with secret placeholders fully expanded. Operators can unintentionally expose internal API keys and policy text while troubleshooting. This is sensitive prompt trace leakage.

title: Cross-tenant vector index mixup returns another customer's chunks
raw_text: A multi-tenant vector service reuses the wrong tenant filter during retrieval, causing cross-tenant semantic recall. Users can receive document chunks from another customer account. This is a severe RAG data isolation vulnerability.

title: Agent task queue replay re-executes previously approved tool actions
raw_text: The asynchronous task queue replays stale approved actions after a worker restart because task signatures are not invalidated. A previously approved destructive tool action can run again unexpectedly. This is an agent workflow replay vulnerability.

title: Document ingestion pipeline trusts OCR footer directives as instructions
raw_text: The ingestion system OCRs scanned PDFs and forwards extracted text directly into downstream summarization prompts. Hidden footer text in a scanned page injects hostile directives into the summarizer. This is prompt injection through document ingestion.

title: Model output parser treats markdown links as executable commands
raw_text: A downstream parser converts model-generated markdown links into executable system actions without user confirmation. An attacker can induce the model to emit crafted links that trigger unsafe operations. This is unsafe output handling in an LLM workflow.

title: Notebook assistant exposes previous user context in shared kernel
raw_text: A shared notebook assistant reuses kernel context across users and exposes prior conversation data in subsequent generations. The flaw leaks prompts, code, and analysis snippets between sessions. This is cross-session context leakage.

title: Security blog roundup on AI model market growth
raw_text: An industry blog summarizes model pricing, enterprise adoption, and new features in AI office products. It does not describe any exploit, attack path, leakage, bypass, or security vulnerability.

title: New vector database release notes
raw_text: The project announces better indexing throughput and lower latency for hybrid search. The article contains no discussion of exploitation, data leakage, prompt injection, or unauthorized access.
